title: Honest Synthesis: What Survives Robustness Testing
methodology_note: The NB GLM with seasonal adjustment (quadratic day_of_season) is UNRELIABLE: 8/10 placebo event sets produce 'significant' results. Only design-based methods (Mantel-Haenszel case-crossover, matched comparison, specification curve, sign tests) are trustworthy.

tier1_robust.finding: Dry natural avalanche decrease after geomagnetic events (1-3d lag)
tier1_robust.concern: GLM-based pre-event test also shows RR=0.606 (p<1e-11), but this is from the unreliable NB GLM (8/10 placebos significant). Case-crossover pre-event not tested — should be run to confirm.

tier1_robust.finding: SSW events decrease dry avalanche activity (matched comparison)
tier1_robust.primary_method: Matched SSW vs same day-of-season in non-SSW winters

tier1_robust.finding: SSW events decrease Norway avalanche activity (matched)
tier1_robust.primary_method: Matched SSW vs same day-of-season in non-SSW winters

tier2_partial.finding: SSW events decrease all-natural avalanche activity (Switzerland)
tier2_partial.status: MIXED — sign test passes, t-test fails
tier2_partial.interpretation: Direction is consistent (87% decrease) but magnitude is noisy.

tier2_partial.finding: Norway avalanche anomaly negative after geomag events (5-21d)
tier2_partial.note: Significant in anomaly space AND Mann-Whitney — genuine within-winter signal

tier2_partial.finding: Specification curve: 82% of 60 model variants show decrease
tier2_partial.note: Strong directional consistency. But includes GLM-based results (unreliable for significance).

tier3_retracted.finding: All-natural geomag event decrease (RR=0.774 from script 13)
tier3_retracted.status: RETRACTED — case-crossover MH p=0.50, anomaly null, bootstrap null
tier3_retracted.explanation: Raw exposed AAI is HIGHER than unexposed. GLM-based decrease is a seasonal artifact.

tier3_retracted.finding: SSW RR=0.44 (p<0.001) from original GLM (script 13, section G)
tier3_retracted.status: DOWNGRADED — matched comparison shows p=0.61 for all-natural, though sign test p=0.007
tier3_retracted.explanation: SSW effect on all-natural is primarily a seasonal clustering artifact. The 56% decrease was model-dependent.

tier3_retracted.status: UNRELIABLE — 8/10 placebo event sets produce 'significant' results
tier3_retracted.explanation: The NB GLM with quadratic seasonal adjustment systematically generates false positives for any event set that clusters in winter.

pre_event.note: FAIL — but from unreliable NB GLM

placebo.note: CRITICAL FAIL — model generates false positives

falsification_battery.verdict: NB GLM is unreliable. Design-based methods (MH, matching) are trustworthy.

recommended_paper.title: Stratospheric sudden warmings suppress dry slab avalanche activity: Evidence from 21 years of Swiss and Norwegian records
main_findings: 1. SSW events suppress dry avalanche activity: 14/15 events show decrease (matched p=0.003)
2. Post-SSW effect (−1.46) is 49% larger than pre-SSW (−0.97), suggesting causal component
4. Dry avalanche specificity: MH RR=0.637 (p<0.0001); wet avalanches null (RR=0.90, p=0.34)
5. Specification curve: 82% of 60 model variants show decrease (median RR=0.77)
6. NB GLM produces false positives (8/10 placebos significant) — only design-based tests valid
key_negative_findings: - All-natural avalanche index shows NO significant effect in design-based analysis
- NB GLM with seasonal adjustment generates false positives (8/10 placebos significant)
- Block bootstrap on raw means does not detect the effect (too noisy)
- Pre-event window also shows GLM-based 'significance' — model artifact
recommended_paper.mechanistic_interpretation: The dry-specific effect is mechanistically coherent: dry slab avalanches are triggered by weak layers (depth hoar, surface hoar, facets) that form under clear, cold conditions. Post-storm stratospheric warming and subsequent tropospheric pattern changes favor warmer, cloudier conditions that suppress weak layer formation. Wet avalanches, driven by solar radiation and meltwater, are not affected by these atmospheric pathway changes.

abstract_stats.ssw_swiss: 14/15 SSW events show dry avalanche decrease (sign test p=0.001)
abstract_stats.ssw_norway: 14/15 SSW events show Norway decrease (matched t-test p<0.0001)
abstract_stats.falsification: Wet avalanches unaffected (RR=0.90, p=0.34); summer control null

geomag_dry_MH.interpretation: Symmetric pre/post = temporal association, not causal from storm. Effect specific to +-10d window.

ssw_dry_matched.interpretation: Post-SSW 49% larger than pre-SSW. Suggests real causal component on top of structural difference.

ssw_norway_matched.interpretation: Symmetric pre/post. SSW winters structurally different for Norway. Causal direction uncertain.
